div.head: 209. Telegram From the Department of State to the Embassy in IndonesiaSource: Department of State, Central Files, 756D.5–MSP/2–657. Confidential. Drafted and approved in FE and cleared with SPA.
div.p: 1367. Advise soonest whether Embassy has since your 1961Telegram 1961 from Djakarta, February 6, stated that the Embassy had not informed the Indonesian Government of U.S. approval of a $10 million line of credit for Indonesia, in addition to the $15 million previously approved for fiscal year 1957. (Ibid.) informed Indos of U.S. aid offer increase from fifteen to twenty-five million. Otherwise believe offer should be withheld at least pending further clarification political developments and Embassy’s recommendations U.S. attitude aid policy.The Embassy replied in telegram 2120 from Djakarta, February 25: “Have not informed Indonesia US aid offer increase from 15 to 25 million. Thoroughly agree with Department that offer of increase be withheld pending clarification political development.” (Ibid., 756D.5–MSP/2–2557)
